title: Hibbertia stelligera
taxonomy: Hibbertia stelligera is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Dilleniaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the bellows flowers or dillenias. Within the Dilleniaceae family, Hibbertia stelligera is further classified into the genus Hibbertia, which comprises about 100 species of flowering plants. The species Hibbertia stelligera is native to Australia, where it can be found growing in a variety of habitats. As a member of the Dilleniaceae family, Hibbertia stelligera is characterized by its unique bell-shaped flowers and distinctive foliage.
uses: Hibbertia stelligera, also known as the starflower, has been traditionally used in Australian herbal medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and stems are used to create a topical cream or infusion to treat wounds and skin irritations. Some studies suggest that Hibbertia stelligera may possess antioxidant and antifungal properties, which could contribute to its potential medicinal uses.
